subject: Geography
title: The Congo
description: Learn The Congo in one minute.

hook.text: The Congo is the deepest river on Earth, plunging over 220 meters in places.

buildup.text: It flows through the world's second-largest rainforest, an area the size of Western Europe.

discovery.text: Its rapids are so fierce that fish evolved separately on each bank.

twist.text: Conrad's 'Heart of Darkness' made the Congo a symbol of colonial horror.

climax.text: The Congo could generate enough hydropower to electrify all of sub-Saharan Africa.

punchline.text: Earth's deepest river hides untapped power.

quiz.question: What makes the Congo River unique among world rivers?
options: It is the deepest river on Earth
